CircleTel · Internal Training · June 2026

Unjani Clinic Onboarding

How to get a clinic from first contact to billing-ready — and what happens at every step, for the nurse and for you.

Audience  Sales administrators · Service delivery team Price  R450 + VAT = R517.50 / month / clinic
Open the dashboard in staging ↗
The big picture

What onboarding actually is

Before we can bill a clinic, the nurse completes a short online setup wizard on their phone. They tap a personal WhatsApp link, confirm details, add banking + a debit-order mandate, and upload documents. Your job is the four verbs below.

Send

the onboarding link to the nurse's WhatsApp

Guide

them through the 6-step wizard (~5 minutes)

Vet

the uploaded documents in the vetting queue

Confirm

the debit order is active and billing is live

One clinic = one setup = one R450 account. A nurse who runs more than one clinic does the setup once per clinic — even on the same phone number. Each clinic is billed separately.

Know which one you have

Two kinds of clinic

Existing clinic

Already connected (the ~20 live clinics)

Needs: just the setup wizard → billing
Billing starts: as soon as setup is done + debit order active
Action: go straight to the 5 steps

New clinic

Not yet installed (e.g. Delmas was)

Needs: coverage check → install → THEN wizard
Billing starts: only after the clinic is installed + activated
Action: do NOT send a link until install is done

First question on any clinic: is it already live, or brand new? The system won't bill a dormant clinic, but the correct order is still on you — never send a billing link before installation.

The process · new clinics · service delivery

New clinic — step by step

Coverage check — is the site feasible, and with which product (SkyFibre, Tarana fixed wireless)?
Create the clinic in the system — done by the CircleTel tech team (billing-safe: it cannot be invoiced yet). It then appears on the dashboard like any other clinic.
Install & activate — site survey → schedule → install → activate. Activation is what starts billing, pro-rated from that day.
Then the normal 5 actions — send invite → guide nurse → vet docs → mandate → service order. Identical to an existing clinic from here.

The order matters: never send a billing setup link before the install is done. The system won't bill an unactivated clinic even if you slip — but the process rule is still coverage → install → activate → wizard.

Open the Register view (click "Register") ↗

Where new clinics come from

The network register on the dashboard lists all 253 Unjani clinics from the MSA savings schedule — current ISP, what they pay today, and what they save on the R450 MSA fee.

253

clinics in the register

132

save money vs their current ISP

R32 850

net saving across the network, per month

R2 050

biggest single saving (Lens Ext 10, vs MTN R2 500)

Prioritise by saving

The register is sorted by monthly saving — clinics with big green numbers are the easiest sells and the first calls. Red numbers (94 clinics pay less today) need a value conversation, not a price pitch.

The process · sales administrators

Your 5 actions (existing clinic)

Confirm  the clinic's WhatsApp number is correct on file.
Send  the invite — Admin → Clinic Onboarding → Send invite on the clinic's row.
Guide  the nurse through the 6-step wizard (~5 min on their phone).
Vet  the uploaded documents in Admin → Document Vetting.
Confirm  billing is live once docs are approved + the debit order is active.
Open Clinic Onboarding ↗ Open Document Vetting ↗
What lands on the nurse's phone

The WhatsApp invite — existing vs new clinic

Existing clinic

Straight to the point — the official template from Circle Tel SA (+27 84 773 9467) with the green Start setup button.

New clinic (after install)

Your personal heads-up first — install done, Wi-Fi live — then the same template. Script A in the guide (§12).

Coach the nurse to expect it: the message comes from "Circle Tel SA", names their exact clinic, and the button is the only thing they need to tap. If they're wary, the heads-up message from you is what builds the trust.

What the nurse sees

The 6-step wizard

Clinic details — confirm pre-filled name, account no., contact, address.
Business details — entity name, CIPC reg number, VAT yes/no.
Banking & debit order — account, branch, tick DebiCheck consent.
Documents — upload CIPC, VAT, bank confirmation, ID, proof of address.
Service order — pick payment date, accept the 24-month term at R450 + VAT.
Done — sees CircleTel account number + what happens next.

Watch step 3: the bank account-holder name must match the clinic's registered name — name mismatches are the #1 cause of debit-order rejection.

Open the nurse wizard — training clinic ↗

Single-use training link (valid to 18 Jun). Spare link on the Live Demo slide. Best shown on a phone.

Right after the wizard · step 3's other half

The DebiCheck mandate SMS (from NetCash)

Minutes after the nurse submits the wizard, NetCash — not CircleTel — sends an SMS from a generic number with a short link and a one-time code. The nurse taps the link, enters the code, and signs the debit-order mandate. Their bank may then ask them to approve the DebiCheck in their banking app.

SMS arrives — short.surf link + 6-digit code, "requested by Circle Tel SA".
Tap & sign — open the link, enter the code. The wizard's details are prefilled; the nurse confirms and signs. NetCash validates the bank account number in real time — typos block the Next button, so it must be the real account number.
Bank approval — approve the DebiCheck prompt in their banking app if asked.

Coach this hard — it looks like phishing. Unknown number, shortened link, a code to enter. Tell the nurse in your heads-up message to expect it: it's the official NetCash DebiCheck process and the message will say "requested by Circle Tel SA". If they ignore it, the debit order never activates and billing can't start.

Real SMS from the training run-through. On production it reads "Circle Tel SA" (the "Test account" suffix is staging's NetCash profile).

If the SMS never arrives

Check the phone number captured in the wizard, then resend the mandate from the clinic's row (Docs approved → "Send mandate link"). Stuck mandates → CircleTel tech team.

Admin → Document Vetting · sales administrators

Vetting documents — your checks

DocumentApprove if…
CIPC company registrationLegible, entity name + reg number match what they typed
VAT certificate (if VAT = Yes)Shows the VAT number they entered
Bank confirmation / statementSame account-holder name + number; ≤ 3 months old
Owner / Director IDClear SA ID or passport of the owner
Proof of business addressRecent (≤ 3 months), shows the clinic address

#1 rejection cause: bank account-holder name ≠ registered clinic name. Check this before approving — it's what kills the debit order. Reject with a clear reason — the nurse is told exactly what to fix and re-uploads just that one document.

Open the vetting queue ↗
Reading the dashboard

Pipeline stages — what to do

StageMeaningDo
Awaiting inviteNothing sent yetSend invite (button on the row)
InvitedLink sent, wizard not finishedSend reminder after a few days
Docs submittedWizard done — in the vetting queueVet documents
Changes requestedOne or more docs rejectedPhone the nurse — they re-upload just that doc
Docs approvedDocs OK, awaiting debit orderRemind nurse to approve the DebiCheck / resend mandate link
Mandate activeDebit order liveIssue the service order / schedule install
Ready to installDocs + debit order doneRaise service order — billing follows

When in doubt, click the orange button. Every clinic row has one Next-Action button that does the right thing for its stage.

The common mistake

Nurses who run 2+ clinics

Each clinic is a separate account with its own link. A nurse with two clinics gets two separate WhatsApp links — even if both go to the same number. Each link is locked to one clinic, so finishing one never affects the other.

Example:  Lesedi Mmoneng runs Barcelona and Delmas → two links, two setups, two R450 bills. Each WhatsApp names its own clinic.

Your job: make sure they understand they must complete the wizard for each clinic — one is not enough.

The handover · service delivery team

Where service delivery takes over

Sales admin owns the pipeline up to Mandate active. From there the clinic is yours: schedule → install → activate — then hand it back to billing.

1 · Service order raised

"Issue service order" on the dashboard generates the Service Order PDF and emails it to the clinic. No second signature needed — the nurse already accepted terms in the wizard.

2 · Schedule

Contact the nurse and agree an install window that suits the clinic; book the technician (and site survey if needed). Confirm the date back to the nurse on WhatsApp.

3 · Install & activate

Complete the install, test the connection, then activate the service. Existing clinics skip straight to activation. Activation starts the 24-month term and billing — first invoice pro-rated from this date.

4 · Confirm with the nurse

After activation, confirm Wi-Fi is up and the nurse knows support channels (082 487 3900). The clinic shows as billing-ready on the dashboard.

Never activate before the mandate is active. Activation starts billing — if the debit order isn't live yet, the first invoice has nothing to collect against.

Handling the common ones

Top FAQs

Nurse didn't get the WhatsApp?

Check the number is correct + WhatsApp-enabled, then re-send (issues a fresh link). Fallback: SMS it.

Link expired / "already used"?

Send a new one — links are 7-day, single-use.

When are they charged?

Billing starts only on service activation — nothing before. First invoice is pro-rated from the activation date; docs approved + debit order active are still required.

Debit order won't activate?

Nurse must sign the NetCash SMS mandate (short link + code), then approve the DebiCheck at their bank. If rejected, it's usually a name mismatch — fix banking & redo.

Nurse worried about bank details?

Reassure: it's the official CircleTel + NetCash DebiCheck process, approved at their own bank.

Copy-paste WhatsApp scripts

Ready-made scripts live in the full guide (§12): heads-up, reminder, expired link, re-upload, DebiCheck nudge, billing-active, bank reassurance.

Live demo

Practice safely — the training clinic

Practice clinic

"Unjani Clinic - Training Demo" (CT-2026-09999). It can never be billed and is deleted after training.

Demo flow

Open the magic link on a phone → walk the 6 wizard steps → watch the clinic move across the dashboard → vet its documents → confirm the mandate → issue the service order.

Messages

Every WhatsApp and email the system sends during the demo goes to the trainer's phone — you'll see exactly what a nurse receives.

⚠ Careful

Real clinics appear on the same dashboard. During practice, only ever click buttons on the Training Demo row — and never click "Send pending invites" (it WhatsApps every real clinic not yet invited).

Nurse wizard — link 1 ↗ Spare link 2 ↗
Admin dashboard ↗ Vetting queue ↗ Admin login ↗

Wizard links are single-use and valid to 18 Jun 2026 — ask for fresh ones per session. Log in to admin before the session starts.

Keep this open

Quick reference

Price & term

R450 ex VAT · R517.50 incl · per clinic / month · 24-month contract · 1st invoice pro-rated from activation · early cancellation = liable for remaining term

Onboarding sender

WhatsApp +27 84 773 9467 (orange "Start setup" button)

Link

Valid 7 days · single-use · re-send issues a fresh one

Required docs

CIPC · VAT (if registered) · bank confirmation · owner ID · proof of address

Where things live

Pipeline: Admin → Clinic Onboarding
Documents: Admin → Document Vetting

Billing starts

Docs approved + debit order active (+ installed & activated, for new clinics)

Support nurses know: 082 487 3900 · contactus@circletel.co.za · Mon–Fri 8am–5pm. Technical / coverage / stuck mandates → CircleTel tech team.
